data.title: Binance Launchpool Introduces GUNZ (GUN), a Layer-1 for AAA Gaming, with Trading Set for March 31
data.description: Binance has announced GUNZ (GUN) as the 66th project on Binance Launchpool, a blockchain infrastructure project developed by Gunzilla Games designed to support AAA gaming. Users will be able to farm GUN tokens by staking BNB, FDUSD, or USDC over a three-day period starting March 28, 2025, ahead of the token’s listing on Binance.About GUNZ (GUN)GUNZ is a gaming-dedicated Layer-1 blockchain built to power high-performance gaming experiences, offering developers and studios a seamless way to integrate blockchain mechanics into traditional AAA games.Key Launch DetailsFarming Period:Begins: March 28, 2025, 00:00 UTCEnds: March 30, 2025, 23:59 UTCBinance Listing:Trading opens: March 31, 2025, 13:00 UTCAvailable pairs: GUN/USDT, GUN/BNB, GUN/FDUSD, GUN/TRYSeed Tag will be applied to the listingTokenomicsToken Name: GUNZ (GUN)Total Token Supply: 10,000,000,000 GUNLaunchpool Rewards: 400,000,000 GUN (4 percent of total supply)Initial Circulating Supply: 604,500,000 GUN (6.05 percent of total supply)Farming Pools and Rewards DistributionPoolTotal RewardsDaily RewardsHourly Cap Per UserBNB320,000,000 GUN106,666,666 GUN444,444 GUNFDUSD40,000,000 GUN13,333,333 GUN55,555 GUNUSDC40,000,000 GUN13,333,333 GUN55,555 GUNParticipation RequirementsUsers must complete Binance KYC verificationUsers must hold eligible tokens in their Spot Wallets to participateWebpage for farming will be live approximately 12 hours before farming beginsAdditional InformationSmart contract and mainnet explorer will be available at public launchResearch report on GUNZ will be published within 24 hours of this announcement

data.title: Acting Chairman of the U.S. SEC: Financial AI should be regulated in a technology-neutral manner
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission officially released the speech of its acting chairman Mark T. Uyeda at the Financial Industry Artificial Intelligence Roundtable. Mark T. Uyeda said that the U.S. financial market has been making continuous progress by leveraging technological innovation, and various forms of artificial intelligence have been used in financial products and services. The use of these technologies has promoted trading and investment as well as improvements in financial products and processes.
Financial regulators should adopt a technology-neutral regulatory approach and avoid an overly prescriptive approach, as this approach may cause rules to quickly become outdated and repetitive, and compliance adopts a "check box" approach and hinders innovation. If technological advances such as artificial intelligence create potential loopholes in the regulatory structure or indicate the need for additional guidance, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has a responsibility to address these loopholes or provide guidance in a way that encourages innovation while protecting investors. The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission must keep in mind its statutory powers and prioritize effective and cost-effective regulations in this area.

data.title: A newly created wallet withdrew 500 billion PEPE from Binance 10 hours ago
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that according to monitoring by The Data Nerd, a newly created wallet withdrew 500 billion PEPE from Binance 10 hours ago, worth approximately US$4.3 million.

data.title: Greeks.live: The market is in a sideways state with low volatility, and BTC traders are paying attention to Trump's tariff policy on April 2
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Greeks.live Macro Researcher Adam released a Chinese community briefing, which pointed out that the market is in a state of sideways low volatility. Most traders are concerned about the volatility that may be caused by Trump's tariff policy on April 2. The group has different views on the impact of tariffs. Some believe that volatility has absorbed the impact, while others are cautious and ready to deal with large fluctuations. The volatility pattern shows that Bitcoin has fluctuated more on Fridays and Sundays in recent weeks (more than 4%), while Saturday fluctuations are usually smaller (less than 2%).

data.title: Bitget has launched U-based KILO perpetual contracts with leverage ranging from 1 to 20 times
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, Bitget announced that it has launched the U-based KILO perpetual contract with a leverage range of 1-20 times. Contract trading BOT will be opened simultaneously.

data.title: Tether increases its stake in Adecoagro from 51% to 70%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Tether, the issuer of stablecoin USDT, has increased its stake in Latin American agricultural company Adecoagro. It is reported that Tether has increased its stake in Adecoagro from 51% to 70% at a price of $12.41 per share. After the relevant announcement, AGRO's stock price rose more than 7% in pre-market trading to $11.95. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Immunefi: Cryptocurrency industry losses from hacker attacks in Q1 reached $1.64 billion, a record high
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Web3 bug bounty platform Immunefi released data showing that there were 40 hacker attacks in the first quarter of 2025, resulting in a record high of $1.64 billion in cryptocurrency losses. In contrast, the first quarter of 2024 increased by 4.7 times, when hackers stole about $348.3 million, although the number of attacks decreased by 36%, from 63 to 40. Most of the losses came from the $1.46 billion hacking attack on Bybit, while the losses from other attacks were about $176 million, a year-on-year decrease of nearly 50%. (The Block)

data.title: Tether Increases Stake in Latin American Agriculture Firm Adecoagro
data.description: According to PANews, Tether has expanded its investment in the Latin American agricultural company Adecoagro. The announcement reveals that Tether's offer of $12.41 per share will increase its ownership in Adecoagro from 51% to 70%, contingent upon certain conditions being met. Adecoagro's operations are primarily focused on the production of sugar, ethanol, dairy, and crops in Argentina, Brazil, and Uruguay. The company manages 210,400 hectares of farmland and operates several industrial facilities across these countries.
